task.title: Reject exhausted SQLite streams before payload encoding
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/402 by returning the existing stream-version range error before serializing an event when SQLite cannot represent the next version, while retaining the authoritative transactional check.
acceptance_criteria: A stream at SQLite's maximum signed version returns EventLogError::VersionOutOfRange(9223372036854775808) before Serialize::serialize is invoked.
The rejected append writes no event.
The in-transaction append path retains an authoritative checked next-version conversion.
Valid appends and existing validation precedence remain unchanged.
The ordering is documented and the pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused regression with a maximum-version stored row and a serializer that must not run.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test panicked with 'a rejected append must not encode its event', proving EventLog::append invoked caller serialization before discovering that the next version exceeded SQLite's signed integer range.

attempts.summary: Preflighted the checked next-version conversion after expected-version matching and before payload encoding.
attempts.patch: The focused regression returned VersionOutOfRange(9223372036854775808), did not invoke serialization, and confirmed the stored row count remained one.

attempts.summary: Refactored preflight and transactional conversion through one private helper.
attempts.patch: Introduced next_stream_version so the unlocked diagnostic preflight and authoritative in-transaction path use the same increment and SQLite i64 conversion.

attempts.summary: Reviewed and verified the bounded change through squash merge.
attempts.diagnostic: A separate pre-commit diff review found no blocking correctness, concurrency, error-precedence, or test gaps. The focused test, just verify, and cargo doc passed; Rust quality gate run 29328427309 passed; CodeRabbit generated no actionable comments; and pull request 403 squash-merged as 7f8d7a1.

outcome.summary: Append now rejects an exhausted SQLite stream before caller-defined serialization while repeating the checked next-version conversion inside the immediate transaction before insertion.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test event_log rejects_an_exhausted_stream_before_encoding -- --exact
Independent pre-commit diff review for issue 402: no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29328427309 for pull request 403
CodeRabbit review for pull request 403: required status passed with no actionable comments
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0020-exhausted-stream-preflight.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 20 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: An append that cannot possibly fit the persistence representation should fail before invoking expensive, fallible, or panicking caller serialization.

A preflight range check improves deterministic error precedence, but the same conversion must remain inside the immediate transaction because another writer can change the current version after preflight.
Centralizing next-version increment and representation conversion prevents the diagnostic preflight from drifting away from the authoritative write path.
Boundary regressions should assert both that caller code is not invoked and that persistent state remains unchanged.
